source.notes: Imported from Azure DevOps work item 6399

observedSteps: User Story :

As a customer, I want to open the Login page from the Home page and sign in with my credentials so that I can access my authenticated account experience and the additional features available after login.
Application Context : This application is a single page application. The Login page is not directly accessed by URL. The user begins on the Home page at:
http://localhost/washtabui/home
The Login page/view is opened by clicking the person/profile button on the Home page.
After successful authentication, the application returns to the Home page in an authenticated state.

Entry Conditions : Launch the application. Navigate to http://localhost/washtabui/home . Ensure the Home page loads successfully in guest state. Open the Login page by clicking the person/profile button.
Test Credentials : The following valid credentials should be used for successful authentication scenarios:
Valid Username : krisadmin Valid Password : Carwash#1 These valid credentials should be treated as approved test data for positive login scenarios in this workflow.
Login Page Elements : The Login page contains:

Username field Password field Login button Password masking behavior Back button Home button These controls should be modeled as Login page elements in the Login page POM/resource where appropriate, while allowing shared shell navigation controls to be reused from the upstream Home page resource when they are already owned there.

Behavior Rules : The Login page should open only through the Home page person/profile button in this workflow. Username and Password are required fields unless business behavior states otherwise. Password input should be masked by default. Valid credentials should authenticate the user successfully. For positive authentication scenarios, the workflow should use the approved valid credentials:

Username : krisadmin Password : Carwash#1 Invalid credentials should not authenticate the user. Failed login should keep the user on the Login page or in the unauthenticated state, with the expected error feedback displayed. Successful login should transition the application back to the Home page in authenticated state. Leading and trailing spaces in entered values should be handled according to expected business behavior and must be validated. The Back button on the Login page should return the user to the Home page. The Home button on the Login page should return the user to the Home page.
Validation Expectations : The workflow should validate:

Login page opens from the Home page Username, Password, and Login button are visible and interactive Back button is visible and usable on the Login page Home button is visible and usable on the Login page Back button returns the user to the Home page Home button returns the user to the Home page Password is masked by default Successful login using the approved valid credentials transitions the user to authenticated Home state Invalid login displays error feedback and does not authenticate the user Required field validations are displayed correctly Whitespace handling behaves according to business expectation Transition Expectations : After successful login:

The user should no longer remain in the guest Home state The Home page should display authenticated-user UI The person/profile button should be replaced by the logged-in user name Additional authenticated-only features should become available POM Reuse Guidance : Use the Home page resource for opening the Login page from Home and for validating post-login authenticated Home state. Use the Login page resource for entering credentials, clicking Login, verifying Login-specific validations. If Back and Home buttons on the Login page are already owned by the shared or upstream Home page resource, the framework should reuse those existing navigation controls instead of duplicating ownership unnecessarily. Successful login tests should reuse both Home and Login resources in sequence:

Home keyword to open Login Login keyword(s) to authenticate Home keyword(s) to verify authenticated state Approved Test Data Guidance : The Login workflow should use the following approved credentials for successful login scenarios:

Username : krisadmin Password : Carwash#1 These credentials should be represented in the Login page resource or associated approved variables so that positive login scenarios use consistent test data across manual tests and automation tests.
Acceptance Criteria :

Given the user is on the Home page in guest state, when the user clicks the person/profile button, then the Login page should open. Given the user is on the Login page, when the user enters the approved valid username krisadmin and the approved valid password Carwash#1 and clicks Login, then the user should be authenticated successfully and the application should return to the Home page in authenticated state. Given the user is on the Login page, when the user enters an invalid username or invalid password and clicks Login, then login should fail, an error message should be displayed, and the user should remain unauthenticated. Given the user is on the Login page, when the user clicks Login without entering required fields, then required field validation should be shown and login should not proceed. Given the user is on the Login page, when the user leaves Username blank and enters Password and clicks Login, then Username validation should be shown and login should not proceed. Given the user is on the Login page, when the user enters Username and leaves Password blank and clicks Login, then Password validation should be shown and login should not proceed. Given the user is on the Login page, when the user enters a password, then the password should be masked by default. Given the user is on the Login page, when the user enters values with leading or trailing spaces and clicks Login, then the application should handle the values according to expected business behavior and the observed result should be validated. Given the user is on the Login page, when the user clicks the Back button, then the user should be returned to the Home page. Given the user is on the Login page, when the user clicks the Home button, then the user should be returned to the Home page. Given the user logs in successfully using the approved valid credentials, when the application returns to Home, then the logged-in user name and authenticated-only features should be visible.
